steps.title: Use the exterior angles
steps.content:
Extend $BC$ and $DE$ until they meet. Since $AB$ is parallel to $DE$, the exterior angle at $B$ matches the angle formed at the extension of $DE$. Its measure is
$$180-136=44$$

steps.title: Find the other triangle angle
steps.content:
The exterior angle at $D$ is supplementary to the given pentagon angle.
$$180-171=9$$

steps.title: Add the remote angles
steps.content:
Angle $C$ of the pentagon is the exterior angle of the small triangle, so it equals the sum of the two remote interior angles.
$$44+9=53$$
The answer is 53.
